message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: GOVERNANCE §1 lists five canonical gate transitions (incl. pre-analysis checkpoint, reporting, escalation) as guarded; the engine runner wires only two confirmatory gates
DIMENSION: docs vs code drift / over-claims
SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: docs/GOVERNANCE.md:8-27; src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:412-423,458-472 GOVERNANCE.md:14-24
DESCRIPTION: GOVERNANCE §1 enumerates five "canonical transitions a gate guards": pre-analysis checkpoint, lock/pre-registration, review, reporting, and escalation, describing each as a code-enforced gate that "cannot be silently bypassed." The composed runner run_study wires only TWO gates: review-locked-hypotheses (PREREGISTER→CONFIRM, pipeline.py:412) and review-baseline-and-claims (→ADJUDICATE, pipeline.py:458). There is NO pre-analysis-checkpoint gate before INGEST, NO reporting gate before REPORT (confirmed results flow straight into REPORT at pipeline.py:526-533 with no operator review), and NO escalation gate. GOVERNANCE §1 does hedge with "the exact count and labels are an engine convention" and "A concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions," and ADR-0010 §4 is explicit ("There are two gates"). But §1's specific claim that the "Reporting" transition — "confirmed results are reviewed before anything is framed as a conclusion or leaves the analytical layer" — is gated is not honored by the engine; the README's "block/park before each confirmatory step" is accurate, but §1 presents reporting/pre-analysis/escalation as engine-guarded gates they are not.
SUGGESTED FIX: Reconcile GOVERNANCE §1 with the runner: state clearly which gates the engine's run_study enforces (the two confirmatory gates) versus which transitions are conventions a study may add via the LangGraph make_gate_node path. Either add a reporting/pre-analysis gate to run_study or relabel §1's list as "transitions a study MAY gate" with an explicit note that the engine runner enforces gates before each confirmatory step only.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# hc-assay — Governance, Reproducibility & Independence
2	
3	The governance machinery is what lets a single operator produce results a hostile reviewer
4	cannot dismiss. It is engine-level and dataset-agnostic.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. Gates (human-in-the-loop at structural transitions)
9	
10	The analysis cannot cross a structural boundary without an explicit, recorded approval.
11	Gates are **deterministic code** — they cannot be silently bypassed. The canonical
12	transitions a gate guards:
13	
14	- **Pre-analysis checkpoint** — prerequisites verified before any data enters the system
15	  (skeleton runs, split determinism verified, provenance store live).
16	- **Lock / pre-registration** — the data-surfaced or claim-derived hypotheses are locked
17	  and timestamped before any confirmatory step. This is the firewall between exploration
18	  and confirmation.
19	- **Review** — the exploratory characterization and the locked hypotheses are reviewed
20	  before confirmatory results are produced or any held-out / null-tested data is touched.
21	- **Reporting** — confirmed results are reviewed before anything is framed as a conclusion
22	  or leaves the analytical layer.
23	- **Escalation** — any move into new territory (new data, materially different method,
24	  scope expansion) parks for explicit approval rather than auto-proceeding.
25	
26	A concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions; the exact count and labels are an
27	engine convention, not a dataset concern.
28	
29	### Gate interrupt/resume protocol (engine mechanics)
30	
31	A gate node parks the run via an `interrupt` and resumes when the operator's decision is
32	delivered (`{gate_id, decision, rationale}`). The engine enforces three properties so the
33	governance trail is sound; a cloning study must respect the state-shape requirement:
34	
35	- **Correlation guard** — a resumed decision must name the gate currently parked; a stale or
36	  misrouted decision aimed at another gate is refused, not applied to whatever is waiting.
37	- **Recoverable, never bricked** — `interrupt` persists the resume value into the checkpoint
38	  before the node returns, so a malformed/mis-correlated value re-fires a fresh interrupt
39	  (the operator can re-submit) instead of raising and stranding the gate forever. Only an
40	  **approved** decision is terminal (re-entry is a no-op); a **rejected/deferred** gate
41	  re-prompts so a revise→re-review loop works.
42	- **Durable parking** — gate graphs must be compiled with the durable checkpointer
43	  (`compile_graph(..., requires_checkpointer=True)`); without one `interrupt` is a silent
44	  no-op and the gate would never pause. Resume must use the *same* checkpointer (keyed by
45	  `run_id` as `thread_id`).
46	
47	**State-shape requirement for cloners:** gate decisions are returned as
48	`{"gate_decisions": [record]}` partial updates, so a study's graph state must declare
49	`gate_decisions` with a list/append reducer — otherwise each gate would overwrite the prior
50	one and the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision.
51	
52	## 2. Pre-registration
53	
54	Before any confirmatory test runs, the relevant hypotheses are:
55	
56	1. made specific and typed (what is claimed, the test, the data, the decision rule),
57	2. content-hashed and committed,
58	3. **RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority.
59	
60	This is honestly characterized as a **timestamped adaptive design**: methods are fixed
61	first; the data informs the questions; the questions are then locked and timestamped before
62	they are tested. It is not claimed to be "predictions registered before any data."
63	
64	> **Implementation status (engine):** pre-registration is enforced by construction **in the
65	> confirmatory runners** (`adjudicate`, `discover_and_confirm`; ADR-0009). They verify, via a
66	> supplied `TimestampAuthority`, that a hypothesis's proof **binds its content and id** (the
67	> canonical digest of its decision-bearing fields), that the attested **timestamp is
68	> verifiable**, and that the lock **precedes confirmation** — a hand-set sentinel string, a
69	> post-lock content/id swap, a forged proof, or a lock dated at/after the ordering instant are
70	> all refused. (In adjudication the ordering instant is captured *before the baseline build*, so
71	> a claim-derived hypothesis must be locked before the baseline exists; in discovery a
72	> data-surfaced hypothesis is necessarily locked inside the runner, so there the *content*
73	> binding is the load-bearing guarantee — see ADR-0009.) The engine ships one real,
74	> data-sovereign authority (`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the Protocol so a study can
75	> plug an RFC-3161 TSA. Honest scope: the local authority is tamper-evidence *relative to an
76	> on-box secret*, not third-party non-repudiation. The lower-level confirm *primitives*
77	> (`confirm_whole_corpus`/`confirm_unit_level`) gate on the cheap presence predicate by default
78	> and take an optional `authority=` to opt into the full check; a study that confirms outside a
79	> runner must pass it (or call `require_preregistered` itself). `Hypothesis.locked` is only a
80	> presence predicate; `methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered` is the
81	> methodology-grade check.
82	
83	## 3. Provenance
84	
85	An **append-only** audit trail (W3C PROV-DM style) records every action before the next
86	executes. No step can be retroactively removed or reordered. This is the spine of
87	reproducibility and of the independence claim.
88	
89	> **Implementation status (engine):** wired (ADR-0010). `assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`
90	> is an append-only, hash-chained trail; the study runner (`assay_engine.pipeline.run_study`)
91	> records every action — ingest, blind baseline, each locked hypothesis (discovery *and*
92	> claim-derived), every gate decision, each verdict, the scorecard, the report — *before the next
93	> runs*, and verifies the chain before returning. There is no remove/edit/reorder API.
94	> **Integrity, honestly, in two tiers:** the default unkeyed chain is *tamper-evident against
95	> naive tampering and accidental corruption* (a single-entry edit, a reorder, or a deletion is
96	> caught by `verify_records`) but is **not** forgery-proof — a party who controls the serialized
97	> bytes can edit a payload and recompute the whole SHA-256 chain. Pass a `secret=` and the chain
98	> is HMAC-keyed, so the head cannot be recomputed without the secret (a downstream store cannot
99	> silently rewrite and re-seal history); `verify_records` must be given the same secret. An
100	> in-memory trail is still as trustworthy as its process, and non-repudiable third-party
101	> attestation of the trail's *time* is the same pluggable concern as pre-registration (§2),
102	> out of scope here. A caller may pass its own trail to `run_study` so a run that *raises* (a
103	> blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail.
104	>
105	> The synchronous `run_study` path records all of the above into the chained trail by default.
106	> The **durable LangGraph gate path** (`make_gate_node`) records decisions into LangGraph
107	> `gate_decisions` *state*, which is not the hash-chained trail; to bring that path under §3
108	> integrity, wire `make_gate_node(..., recorder=...)` to a `ProvenanceTrail` (the recorder
109	> bridge, #111). Without that bridge, the graph path's gate decisions are graph state, not
110	> tamper-evident trail entries.
111	
112	## 4. Single-operator structural independence
113	
114	Independence from operator bias is achieved structurally, not by a co-reviewer:
115	
116	- **Code gates** the operator cannot silently bypass.
117	- **Append-only provenance** that records the full decision history.
118	- **Timestamped pre-registration** committing to hypotheses before they are tested.
119	- **A complete reproducibility package** published with findings.
120	
121	This is a deliberate methodological position and is stated explicitly in any output.
122	
123	## 5. Data sovereignty
124	
125	All computation, storage, tracing, and experiment tracking run **on-box**. No data,
126	embeddings, traces, or results leave the machine. SaaS observability and any service that
127	would ship analyzed content off-box are disqualified by this constraint, independent of
128	cost.
129	
130	## 6. Reproducibility guarantees (summary)
131	
132	- Deterministic seeds (documented); inputs hashed; model names + versions recorded.
133	- Append-only provenance for every action.
134	- Timestamped pre-registration before confirmation.
135	- Published data + code + configuration + logs sufficient to reconstruct every verdict.
136

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import uuid
33	from contextlib import ExitStack
34	from dataclasses import dataclass
35	from pathlib import Path
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping
37	
38	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
39	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
40	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
42	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
43	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
44	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
45	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
46	    ClaimConfirmer,
47	    SourceScorecard,
48	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
49	)
50	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
51	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
52	    ClaimBlindGuard,
53	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
54	    FirewallViolation,
55	)
56	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
57	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
58	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
59	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
60	    OtelTracer,
61	    Tracer,
62	    bootstrap_tracing,
63	    run_trace_context,
64	)
65	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
66	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
67	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
68	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
69	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
70	
71	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
72	
73	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
74	
75	
76	class IngestionError(ValueError):
77	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
78	
79	
80	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
81	class GateReview:
82	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition."""
83	
84	    gate: str
85	    frm: Phase
86	    to: Phase
87	    summary: str
88	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
89	
90	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
91	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
92	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
93	
94	
95	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
96	
97	
98	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
99	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
100	
101	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
102	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
103	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
104	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
105	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
106	    """
107	    return GateDecision(
108	        approved=True,
109	        gate=review.gate,
110	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
111	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
112	    )
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
116	class StudyPlan:
117	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
118	
119	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
120	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
121	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
122	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
123	    definition).
124	    """
125	
126	    definition: StudyDefinition
127	    source: Path
128	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
129	    authority: TimestampAuthority
130	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
131	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
132	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
133	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
134	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
135	
136	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
137	        modes = self.definition.modes
138	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
139	            missing = [
140	                n
141	                for n, v in (
142	                    ("split", self.split),
143	                    ("discover", self.discover),
144	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
145	                )
146	                if v is None
147	            ]
148	            if missing:
149	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
150	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
151	            missing = [
152	                n
153	                for n, v in (
154	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
155	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
156	                )
157	                if v is None
158	            ]
159	            if missing:
160	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
161	
162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyResult:
165	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
166	
167	    study: str
168	    corpus_fingerprint: str
169	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
170	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
171	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
172	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
173	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
174	    source_version: str | None = None
175	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
176	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
177	
178	
179	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
180	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
181	
182	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
183	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
184	
185	    return _log
186	
187	
188	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
189	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
190	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
191	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
192	
193	
194	def run_study(
195	    plan: StudyPlan,
196	    *,
197	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
198	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
199	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
200	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
201	    clock: Clock | None = None,
202	    secret: bytes | None = None,
203	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
204	) -> StudyResult:
205	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
206	
207	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
208	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
209	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
210	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
211	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
212	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
213	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
214	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
215	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
216	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
217	
218	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
219	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
220	    """
221	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
222	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
223	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
224	    defn = plan.definition
225	    modes = defn.modes
226	    required = required_phases(modes)
227	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
228	    visited: list[Phase] = []
229	    run_id: str | None = None
230	
231	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
232	        visited.append(phase)
233	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
234	
235	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
236	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
237	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
238	            return
239	        try:
240	            fn(tracker, run_id)
241	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
242	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
243	
244	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
245	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
246	        decision = gate_handler(review)
247	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
248	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
249	        trail.record(
250	            "gate",
251	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
252	            gate=review.gate,
253	            approved=approved,
254	            reason=reason,
255	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
256	        )
257	        if not approved:
258	            raise GateError(
259	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
260	            )
261	
262	    trail.record(
263	        "run_start",
264	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
265	        study=defn.name,
266	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
267	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
268	    )
269	    if tracker is not None:
270	        try:
271	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
272	                defn.name,
273	                {
274	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
275	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
276	                },
277	            )
278	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
279	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
280	
281	    # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
282	    # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122). Best-effort.
283	    bootstrap_tracing()
284	    corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
285	    ok = False
286	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
287	    trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
288	    try:
289	        # ---- INGEST ----
290	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
291	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
292	            try:
293	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
294	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
295	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
296	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
297	            if not corpus.units:
298	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
299	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
300	            source_version: str | None = None
301	            if versioner is not None:
302	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
303	            trail.record(
304	                "ingest",
305	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
306	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
307	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
308	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
309	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
310	                source_version=source_version,
311	            )
312	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
313	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
314	                assert plan.split is not None
315	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
316	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
317	                if unknown:
318	                    raise FirewallViolation(
319	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
320	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
321	                    )
322	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
323	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
324	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
325	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
326	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
327	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
328	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
329	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
330	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
331	                    raise FirewallViolation(
332	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
333	                    )
334	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
335	                if ghost:
336	                    raise FirewallViolation(
337	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
338	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
339	                    )
340	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
341	                trail.record(
342	                    "features",
343	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
344	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
345	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
346	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
347	                )
348	
349	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
350	        baseline_instant = _now()
351	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
352	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
353	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
354	            with guard.sealed():
355	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
356	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
357	                raise FirewallViolation(
358	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
359	                    "was not built from this corpus"
360	                )
361	            trail.record(
362	                "baseline",
363	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
364	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
365	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
366	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
367	            )
368	
369	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
370	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
371	
372	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
373	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
374	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
375	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
376	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
377	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
378	
379	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
380	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
381	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
382	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
383	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
384	                trail.record(
385	                    "discovery",
386	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
387	                    n=len(hypotheses),
388	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
389	                )
390	
391	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
392	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
393	                if not held_out.units:
394	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
395	                preregister_instant = _now()
396	                for h in hypotheses:
397	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
398	                        raise FirewallViolation(
399	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
400	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
401	                        )
402	                    vt = require_preregistered(
403	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
404	                    )
405	                    trail.record(
406	                        "preregister",
407	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
408	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
409	                        digest=vt.digest,
410	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
411	                    )
412	                run_gate(
413	                    GateReview(
414	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
415	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
416	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
417	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
418	                        payload={
419	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
420	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
421	                        },
422	                    )
423	                )
424	
425	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
426	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
427	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
428	                for h in hypotheses:
429	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
430	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
431	                        raise FirewallViolation(
432	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
433	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
434	                        )
435	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
436	                    trail.record(
437	                        "verdict",
438	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
439	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
440	                        label=verdict.label.value,
441	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
442	                    )
443	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
444	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
445	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
446	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
447	
448	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
449	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
450	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
451	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
452	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
453	            if not claim_records:
454	                raise ValueError(
455	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
456	                )
457	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
458	            run_gate(
459	                GateReview(
460	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
461	                    frm=prev_phase,
462	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
463	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
464	                    payload={
465	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
466	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
467	                        "claim_fingerprint": defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint(),
468	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
469	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
470	                    },
471	                )
472	            )
473	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
474	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
475	
476	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
477	                    trail.record(
478	                        "preregister",
479	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
480	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
481	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
482	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
483	                    )
484	                    trail.record(
485	                        "verdict",
486	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
487	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
488	                        label=v.label.value,
489	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
490	                    )
491	
492	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
493	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
494	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
495	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
496	                    baseline,
497	                    claim_records,
498	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
499	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
500	                    authority=plan.authority,
501	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
502	                    source_name=defn.name,
503	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
504	                )
505	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
506	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
507	                trail.record(
508	                    "score",
509	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
510	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
511	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
512	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
513	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
514	                )
515	                sc = scorecard
516	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
517	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
518	                track(
519	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
520	                )
521	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
522	                if ar is not None:
523	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
524	
525	        # ---- REPORT ----
526	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
527	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
528	            trail.record(
529	                "report",
530	                "assembled reproducibility package",
531	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
532	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
533	            )
534	
535	        if tuple(visited) != required:
536	            raise FirewallViolation(
537	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
538	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
539	            )
540	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
541	        ok = True
542	    except Exception as exc:
543	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
544	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
545	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
546	        try:
547	            trail.record(
548	                "run_failed",
549	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
550	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
551	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
552	                last_phase=last_phase,
553	            )
554	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — never mask the original failure
555	            pass
556	        raise
557	    finally:
558	        trace_ctx.close()
559	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
560	            try:
561	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
562	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
563	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
564	                pass
565	
566	    return StudyResult(
567	        study=defn.name,
568	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
569	        baseline=baseline,
570	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
571	        scorecard=scorecard,
572	        phases=tuple(visited),
573	        provenance=trail.entries,
574	        source_version=source_version,
575	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
576	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
577	    )
578

content.text: The code matches the assessor's description. Let me verify the supporting docs referenced: ADR-0010 and the README claim.

content.content:
docs/decisions/ADR-0010-engine-composed-study-runner.md:64:   an operator/LangGraph handler can block (raising `GateError`) or park. There are two gates so
docs/hardening/PASS-1.md:183:- **Problem:** There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE §3 states the chained ProvenanceTrail "is the spine… No step can be retroactively removed or reordered," and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list "the append-only trail." But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained ProvenanceTrail: apply_gate_decision takes no trail/recorder and returns a bare dict (PoC confirmed: {'gate_decisions': [{'gate':'review','decision':'rejected','rationale':'no good'}]}), and grep shows no bridge from gate_decisions into ProvenanceTrail (the as_recorder bridge at provenance.py:152 is wired only to the synchronous Gate.evaluate path, not the gatenode). The graph's 'append-only trail' has no prev_hash/entry_hash, no verify_records, and relies entirely on the study correctly declaring a list/add reducer (a documented foot-gun: lines 47-50 warn that a wrong reducer silently drops all but the last decision). So for the durable, cross-process governance path — the one production runs that park for an operator actually use — the most governance-relevant mutations (human approve/reject decisions) are recorded only as unchained, tamper-evident-free state that can be reordered or dropped without detection. The integrity guarantee GOVERNANCE §3 advertises does not hold for this path.

README.md:48:of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that can block/park before each confirmatory
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:209:    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a

50	     and the provenance chain must verify, before a `StudyResult` is returned.
51	
52	   A study supplies the *domain* via a `StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
53	   discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, claims source); the engine owns the *order, the
54	   firewalls, the gate, the provenance, and the tracing*. The runner imports no adapter.
55	
56	3. **Mode-accurate phases.** Building the composition exposed that `required_phases` forced the
57	   discovery spine (DISCOVERY/PREREGISTER/CONFIRM) onto *every* study, including adjudicate-only
58	   ones — which derive hypotheses from claims, not data. The modes are independent, so the spine
59	   is now conditional on `DISCOVERY` mode, mirroring how ADJUDICATE/SCORE are conditional on
60	   adjudication mode. An adjudicate-only study is INGEST→BASELINE→ADJUDICATE→SCORE→REPORT.
61	
62	4. **The governance gate is a pluggable handoff, before *every* confirmatory step.** `run_study`
63	   takes a `gate_handler` (`GateReview → GateDecision`); the default auto-approves and records, and
64	   an operator/LangGraph handler can block (raising `GateError`) or park. There are two gates so
65	   no confirmatory step is ungated (adversarial review #86): `review-locked-hypotheses` before
66	   CONFIRM (discovery), and `review-baseline-and-claims` before ADJUDICATE (adjudication). The
67	   decision's `approved` is snapshotted once and used for both the recorded entry and the
68	   control-flow branch, so it cannot diverge (#94). A caller may pass its own `trail` to
69	   `run_study` so a *blocked* run still leaves an auditable partial trail including the blocking
70	   decision (#92).
71	
72	## Consequences
73	
74	- There is now a single, engine-owned, **runnable** end-to-end workflow, verified both purely
75	  (`tests/test_pipeline.py`, both modes + negative paths) and **live** against a real backend
76	  (`tests/integration/test_pipeline_live.py` — a real OpenTelemetry span fires around every phase
77	  handoff, with an intact provenance chain). The reference adapter (`tests/reference_study.py`) is
78	  the dataset-agnostic demonstration of the workflow.
79	- GOVERNANCE §3 is honoured by construction, not promised. Honest scope: an in-memory trail is as

40	A complete, runnable example (both modes, synthetic data, no backends needed) is in
41	[`examples/minimal_study.py`](examples/minimal_study.py):
42	
43	```bash
44	python examples/minimal_study.py
45	```
46	
47	`gate_handler` is required so governance is a conscious choice: pass `auto_approve` to opt out
48	of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that can block/park before each confirmatory
49	step. See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) for the adapter contract and
50	[docs/METHODOLOGY.md](docs/METHODOLOGY.md) for the method.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## What it does

content.text:
I have confirmed all the facts. Let me assess.

**Confirmed facts:**

1. GOVERNANCE §1 (lines 14-24) enumerates five "canonical transitions a gate guards" and the preamble (lines 10-11) states "The analysis cannot cross a structural boundary without an explicit, recorded approval. Gates are **deterministic code** — they cannot be silently bypassed."

2. The runner `run_study` wires exactly TWO gates: `review-locked-hypotheses` (PREREGISTER→CONFIRM, line 412) and `review-baseline-and-claims` (→ADJUDICATE, line 458).

3. There is NO pre-analysis gate before INGEST (line 289-291 enters INGEST directly), NO reporting gate before REPORT (line 526-533 enters REPORT directly with no `run_gate` call), NO escalation gate.

4. GOVERNANCE §1 *does* hedge (lines 26-27): "A concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions; the exact count and labels are an engine convention, not a dataset concern."

5. ADR-0010 §4 (line 64-66) is explicit: "There are two gates."

6. README (line 47-48) is accurate: "block/park before each confirmatory step."

**Assessment:**

The assessor's facts are all correct. The question is whether this constitutes a genuine production-readiness defect.

This is a docs-vs-code drift finding, and the substance is real but the severity is genuinely low. The hedge in §1 ("A concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions; the exact count and labels are an engine convention") does meaningful work — §1 is explicitly framed as the *menu of canonical transitions a study may map onto*, not a list of gates the engine runner unconditionally fires. The word "canonical" plus "a concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions" signals these are the conceptual transition types, and the engine's two gates each map onto one of them (Lock/pre-registration → `review-locked-hypotheses`; Review → `review-baseline-and-claims`).

However, the assessor's narrower point has teeth: §1 describes each transition in the indicative as a guarantee that holds ("confirmed results **are reviewed** before anything is framed as a conclusion or leaves the analytical layer" for Reporting; "prerequisites **verified** before any data enters" for Pre-analysis). For a study run via the engine's `run_study`, those two transitions are simply not gated — there is no operator review between SCORE and REPORT. So a reader of §1 alone would over-estimate what the shipped runner enforces. This is a real over-claim relative to code, even if mild.

This is genuinely a documentation-accuracy issue, not a behavioral/security defect. The code is correct and the README + ADR-0010 are accurate; only GOVERNANCE §1's framing overstates. That makes it LOW (a docs polish item), not a production-blocking defect. It is real, not a false positive.

Let me confirm there's no escalation/reporting gate anywhere in the engine's runnable path that the assessor might have missed.

content.text:
Confirmed: only the two `run_gate` calls exist at lines 412 and 458. No reporting, pre-analysis, or escalation gate in the engine's runner.

I have enough to render a verdict.

input.reasoning:
Verified against code and docs; the assessor's facts all hold. GOVERNANCE.md §1 (lines 10-24) introduces five "canonical transitions a gate guards" under the absolute preamble "The analysis cannot cross a structural boundary without an explicit, recorded approval. Gates are deterministic code — they cannot be silently bypassed," and describes each transition in the indicative as an enforced guarantee (e.g. Reporting: "confirmed results are reviewed before anything is framed as a conclusion or leaves the analytical layer"; Pre-analysis: "prerequisites verified before any data enters the system"). The engine runner run_study wires exactly TWO gates via run_gate(): review-locked-hypotheses (PREREGISTER->CONFIRM, pipeline.py:412) and review-baseline-and-claims (->ADJUDICATE, pipeline.py:458). grep confirms only those two GateReview/run_gate sites exist. There is no gate before INGEST (entered directly at 289-291), no gate before REPORT (entered directly at 526-533; SCORE flows straight into REPORT with no operator review), and no escalation gate. So for a study run via the shipped engine runner, the §1 "Reporting", "Pre-analysis checkpoint", and "Escalation" transitions are NOT code-enforced gates, contradicting §1's indicative framing.

This is a genuine docs-vs-code over-claim, but it is mild and the surrounding documentation is mostly accurate, which caps severity at LOW: (1) §1 hedges at lines 26-27 — "A concrete study maps its phases onto these transitions; the exact count and labels are an engine convention" — framing the list as canonical transition TYPES a study may map onto, and the two shipped gates each map onto one (Lock/pre-registration and Review). (2) ADR-0010 §4 (lines 64-66) is explicit and correct: "There are two gates so no confirmatory step is ungated." (3) README (lines 47-48) is accurate: "block/park before each confirmatory step." The defect is confined to §1's indicative phrasing overstating which transitions the engine runner unconditionally enforces; it is a documentation-accuracy/over-claim item, not a behavioral, security, or methodological defect — the code is correct and the firewalls/pre-registration it relies on are enforced. Suggested fix (relabel §1 as "transitions a study MAY gate" with a note that run_study enforces gates before each confirmatory step only) is reasonable. Not a false positive; not handled elsewhere in a way that fully neutralizes §1's wording. Severity LOW confirmed (assessor's own LOW is well-calibrated).
input.evidence: docs/GOVERNANCE.md:10-11 (absolute "cannot be silently bypassed" / "deterministic code" framing) and 14-24 (five canonical transitions incl. Pre-analysis, Reporting, Escalation described in the indicative as enforced); hedge at GOVERNANCE.md:26-27. src/assay_engine/pipeline.py: only two run_gate calls — line 412 (review-locked-hypotheses, PREREGISTER->CONFIRM) and line 458 (review-baseline-and-claims, ->ADJUDICATE); INGEST entered ungated at 289-291; REPORT entered ungated at 526-533 (no run_gate between SCORE and REPORT); grep for run_gate/GateReview returns only definitions plus those two call sites. Counter-evidence (accurate docs): docs/decisions/ADR-0010-engine-composed-study-runner.md:64-66 ("There are two gates so no confirmatory step is ungated"); README.md:47-48 ("block/park before each confirmatory step"). Net: real LOW documentation over-claim localized to GOVERNANCE §1's indicative phrasing.

content.text:
Verdict recorded: this is a real but LOW-severity documentation over-claim, not a behavioral defect.

Summary: The assessor's facts are all correct and verified against the code. GOVERNANCE.md §1 (lines 10-24) presents five canonical transitions (pre-analysis, lock/pre-registration, review, reporting, escalation) under an absolute "cannot be silently bypassed / deterministic code" framing, describing each in the indicative as enforced. But `src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` wires only two gates (lines 412 and 458 — the only `run_gate` call sites), with INGEST and REPORT entered ungated. So §1's "Reporting," "Pre-analysis," and "Escalation" are not engine-enforced gates.

It is a genuine docs-vs-code over-claim (is_real=true), but capped at LOW because: §1 hedges at lines 26-27 that these are conventions a study maps onto; ADR-0010 §4 is explicit and correct ("There are two gates"); and the README is accurate ("before each confirmatory step"). The defect is confined to §1's indicative phrasing — a documentation-accuracy item, not a security/behavioral/methodological flaw. The suggested fix (relabel §1 as "transitions a study MAY gate" with a note that the runner enforces gates before confirmatory steps only) is sound.
